obligations.title: Register as a Data Controller of Major Importance
obligations.description: Organisations processing personal data of Nigerian residents at scale must register with NDPC as a Data Controller/Processor of Major Importance (DCMI/DPMI) and file a designation within 6 months of qualifying.

obligations.title: Appoint a Data Protection Officer
obligations.description: Every Data Controller of Major Importance must designate a qualified Data Protection Officer and publish their contact details in the organisation's privacy notice.

obligations.title: Conduct Data Protection Impact Assessments
obligations.description: Before any high-risk processing (profiling, large-scale sensitive data, automated decision-making), perform a DPIA documenting risk and mitigation. Refresh at least annually.

obligations.title: Notify personal-data breaches within 72 hours
obligations.description: Report any breach likely to result in risk to data subjects to NDPC within 72 hours of becoming aware, and to affected individuals without undue delay where risk is high.

obligations.title: File annual compliance audit return
obligations.description: Data Controllers of Major Importance must commission a Data Protection Compliance Audit by an NDPC-licensed DPCO and file the return annually.

obligations.title: Respond to data subject rights requests within 30 days
obligations.description: Respond to access, rectification, erasure, restriction, portability, and objection requests without undue delay and at most 30 days of receipt.

obligations.title: Establish cross-border transfer safeguards
obligations.description: Transfers of personal data outside Nigeria require an adequacy decision, binding corporate rules, standard contractual clauses, or an explicit derogation. Maintain the transfer register.

obligations.title: Publish and maintain a compliant privacy notice
obligations.description: Publish a privacy notice in plain language covering purposes, lawful basis, retention, rights, and transfer safeguards; review annually.

obligations.title: Maintain a Record of Processing Activities (RoPA)
obligations.description: Keep an up-to-date RoPA covering controller/processor identity, purposes, categories of data subjects, recipients, transfers, retention periods, and security measures.

obligations.title: Operate a demonstrable consent management system
obligations.description: Where processing relies on consent, record who consented, when, to what, and provide an equally easy mechanism to withdraw.
